title: Privacy Policy
content:
1. Introduction

The University of Studingham ("the University", "we", "us") is committed to protecting the privacy and security of all users who access the University Portal ("the Portal").

This Privacy Policy explains how personal data is collected, used, stored, and safeguarded. By accessing or using the Portal, users agree to the practices described in this policy.

2. Data Controller

The University of Studingham acts as the Data Controller, responsible for determining the purposes and methods of processing personal data through the Portal.

Users may contact the University regarding privacy concerns through the official University email and communication channels provided in the Portal.

3. Purpose of the Portal

The Portal is designed to provide secure, reliable access to:

  • Academic resources
  • Learning materials
  • Library facilities
  • Timetables and course details
  • File submissions and downloads
  • University announcements and communication tools
  • Administrative and academic management services

4. Types of Users

The Portal is used by:

  • Students
  • Tutors
  • Administrative staff

5. Information We Collect

5.1 Personal Identification Information

  • Full name
  • University email address
  • Student or Staff ID number
  • Login credentials (encrypted passwords)

5.2 Academic and Activity Information

  • Course enrolments and lecture details
  • Grades, transcripts, and assessment results
  • Attendance records
  • Timetables and meeting schedules
  • Student finance details
  • Library bookings
  • File submissions, downloads, and course progress indicators
  • Other educational interactions within the Portal

5.3 How Data Is Collected

Data is collected through:

  • Registration forms completed during student enrolment or staff onboarding
  • Automatic logging and system updates as users interact with Portal services

6. Purpose of Data Processing

We process personal data to:

  • Authenticate and verify users
  • Provide access to course materials and university services
  • Ensure platform security and prevent unauthorised access
  • Support teaching, learning, and academic administration
  • Maintain accurate institutional and academic records

7. Legal Basis for Processing

7.1 Contractual / Service Requirement

Processing is required to:

  • Provide access to academic resources
  • Display grades, progress indicators, and assignment submissions
  • Facilitate communication between students, tutors, and staff

7.2 Legal Obligations

The University must maintain certain academic and administrative records in compliance with educational laws and regulatory requirements.

7.3 Public Task / Educational Mission

As part of its educational role, the University processes personal data to manage teaching, academic administration, and student services.

8. Data Sharing

The Portal does not share personal data with any external third parties.

If future systems or integrations require data sharing, this section will be updated accordingly.

9. Security Measures

To protect all user data, the Portal uses:

  • Encrypted passwords
  • Secure authentication, including email-based verification during login
  • Role-based access controls
  • Encrypted data transfers
  • Regular system monitoring and internal security audits
  • Protection against unauthorised access, misuse, or modification

The University prioritises technical and organisational security measures to safeguard all stored information.

10. Automated Decision-Making

The Portal does not use automated decision-making or profiling that impacts users.

The only automated processing performed includes:

  • Basic course progress calculations
  • Completion percentages
  • Class schedule organisation

These functions do not produce decisions affecting user rights or access.

11. Children / Under-16 Use

The Portal is not intended for users under the age of 16. Only enrolled students, tutors, and administrative staff are permitted access.

12. Data Storage and Retention

12.1 Storage

All personal data is stored securely within the University’s protected database systems. No data is transferred outside the University.

12.2 Retention

  • Students: Data is retained for the duration of their studies
  • Staff: Data is retained for the duration of their employment or involvement

After these periods, data is securely deleted or anonymised unless legal obligations require longer retention.

13. Data Breach Protocol

In the event of a data breach:

  • The University will investigate immediately
  • Affected users will be notified
  • Authorities will be informed where legally required

The University complies with applicable data protection regulations regarding breach response.

14. User Rights

All Portal users have the right to:

  • Request access to their personal data
  • Request correction of inaccurate or outdated information
  • Request deletion of their data where legally permissible
  • Object to improper handling of their information
  • File a complaint with the University’s Data Protection contact

15. Cookies and Tracking

The Portal does not use cookies or tracking technologies.

16. Policy Updates

This Privacy Policy may be updated periodically. Users will be notified of any significant changes, and continued use of the Portal constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

17. Contact Information

For privacy-related questions, data requests, or concerns, users may contact the University of Studingham through the official communication channels provided within the Portal.
